title: After lawsuit, Secretary of Commonwealth agrees to produce legally mandated report on voting in jail
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Secretary William Galvin agrees to produce reports on voting in jail after lawsuit in Massachusetts.
Reports will detail jail voting access, mandated by the 2022 VOTES Act, following missed elections in 2024 and 2025.
Galvin's office cited technical issues for delays and commits to improvements before the September statewide primary.

title: New Patriots fullback Reggie Gilliam sees a similarity between Drake Maye and former MVP
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Reggie Gilliam, former Buffalo Bills fullback, signed a three-year contract with the New England Patriots.
Gilliam compares new Patriots quarterback Drake Maye to former MVP Josh Allen, citing their similar build and potential.
Gilliam aims to enhance New England's running game, utilizing his blocking skills to support Rhamondre Stevenson and TreVeyon Henderson.

title: Red Sox infielder Romy Gonzalez undergoes shoulder surgery; Boston signs lefty reliever ​
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Romy Gonzalez undergoes shoulder surgery, will miss at least the first two months of 2026 season.
Red Sox sign left-handed reliever Danny Coulombe to a one-year contract to strengthen bullpen depth.
Coulombe posted a 2.30 ERA over 55 appearances in 2025, split between the Twins and Rangers.

title: High school student allegedly killed Danvers woman in her home in ‘random’ attack, DA says
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Anthony DeMayo, high school senior, charged with murdering Janet Swallow, 68, in her Danvers home, Essex DA reports.
DeMayo found with a knife showing a reddish brown stain, arrested on Standish Street, Lynn by local police.
No connection found between Swallow and DeMayo; incident believed to be a random act of violence, DA Tucker states.

title: Boston police seeking suspect who allegedly assaulted pregnant woman in Roxbury laundromat
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police seek suspect who allegedly assaulted a pregnant woman at 104 Warren St. laundromat on March 1.
Suspect described as a 6'0" light-skinned male wearing a Yankees hat, beige hoodie, and blue pants.
Investigators also search for a female witness at the scene; public tips can be submitted via CrimeStoppers.

title: Fired Mass. cop arrested in N.H. for allegedly impersonating a police officer
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Mikel Beltran, 37, was arrested in Londonderry, N.H. for allegedly impersonating a police officer with flashing blue SUV lights.
Beltran, a former Lawrence cop, was fired in March 2025 after being placed on leave in September 2024.
Beltran is suing the Lawrence police for allegedly conducting an unconstitutional search of his phone, leading to his firing.

title: NFL insider believes Eagles will lower asking price to Patriots in A.J. Brown trade
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots signed Romeo Doubs but are interested in trading for Eagles' A.J. Brown this offseason.
NFL insider Mike Giardi suggests Eagles might lower trade demands for A.J. Brown to the Patriots.
Eagles face a cap penalty if trading Brown before June 1, encouraging possible post-June negotiations.

title: Truck catches fire on Mass. Pike during early-morning commute, causing lane shutdowns
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A truck caught fire on the Massachusetts Turnpike in Westborough, closing westbound lanes during the morning commute.
The fire was reported around 8 a.m. near the 102.4 mile marker and extinguished by 8:30 a.m.
All westbound lanes reopened by 1:30 p.m.; no injuries were reported and the fire's cause is under investigation.

title: New Patriots WR Romeo Doubs explains why he chose New England in free agency
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Romeo Doubs signs a four-year, up to $80 million deal with the New England Patriots.
The wide receiver chose New England partly to play alongside quarterback Drake Maye.
Doubs was Green Bay Packers' top receiver in 2025 with 55 catches, 724 yards, and six touchdowns.

title: SUV owner experiences a serious – and not uncommon – engine issue
author: John Paul
bullets: The 2018 Hyundai Tucson owner experienced engine failure at 60,000 miles; the engine replacement was covered by warranty.
Hyundai, Kia, Toyota, and Chevrolet vehicles face engine issues due to improperly machined bearings causing engine knock and power loss.
Toyota recalls hundreds of thousands of Tundra/Lexus vehicles; General Motors recalls trucks/SUVs over potential engine defects.
